input.prompt: This .NET solution has grown organically and the build infrastructure needs attention. Review all project files and suggest how to improve the build organization. Focus on reducing duplication, improving maintainability, and applying MSBuild best practices.

1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2. 
3.   <PropertyGroup>
4.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
5.     <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
6.     <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
7.     <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
8.     <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
9.     <Company>Contoso</Company>
10.     <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
11.   </PropertyGroup>
12. 
13.   <ItemGroup>
14.     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.0" />
15.     <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
16.   </ItemGroup>
17. 
18.   <PropertyGroup>
19.     <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
20.   </PropertyGroup>
21. 
22.   <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
23.     <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
24.   </Target>
25. 
26. </Project>
27.

 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
 
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
     <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
     <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
     <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
     <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
     <Company>Contoso</Company>
     <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.0" />
     <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
   <PropertyGroup>
     <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
     <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
   </Target>

     <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
   <ItemGroup>
     <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Api\ContosoApp.Api.csproj" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>

data.content:
Here's my analysis of the build infrastructure across all 5 projects:

---

## Issues Found

### 1. Massive property duplication (all 5 projects)
Every `.csproj` repeats identical settings:
```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

### 2. No Central Package Management — version drift already present
| Package | Core | Api | Services | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---------|------|-----|----------|------------|-----------|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | 8.0.0 | 8.0.0 | **8.0.1** | — | — |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | — | 17.10.0 | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | — | — | — | 2.8.1 | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | — | — | — | 2.8.1 | **2.5.6** |

### 3. Duplicated custom target
`PrintBuildInfo` is copy-pasted into all 3 source projects.

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property
Set identically in all 3 source projects.

### 5. No `global.json`
SDK version isn't pinned — builds may behave differently across machines.

---

## Recommended Solution

### A. Add `Directory.Build.props` (repo root)
Centralizes all shared properties. Use conditions to apply test-specific defaults:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source project defaults -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test project defaults -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### B. Add `Directory.Build.targets` (repo root)
Moves the shared custom target out of individual projects:

```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

### C. Add `Directory.Packages.props` (Central Package Management)
Single source of truth for all package versions:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` uses `<PackageReference Include="xunit" />` (no `Version`).

### D. Add `global.json`
```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.300",
    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
  }
}
```

### E. Resulting simplified project files
After all of the above, `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` shrinks to:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And `ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj` becomes:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Summary of Impact

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Lines of XML across projects | ~130 | ~55 |
| Places to update TFM | 5 | 1 |
| Places to update a package version | per-project | 1 |
| Version drift bugs | 4 | 0 (enforced) |

Would you like me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the build infrastructure. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **No `Directory.Build.props`** - Every project repeats the same properties (TargetFramework, Nullable, ImplicitUsings, TreatWarningsAsErrors, EnforceCodeStyleInBuild, Company, Authors).

2. **No `Directory.Packages.props` (Central Package Management)** - Package versions are hardcoded in each project, and there are version inconsistencies:
   - `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`: 8.0.0 in Core and Api, but 8.0.1 in Services
   - `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`: 17.10.0 in Core.Tests, 17.9.0 in Api.Tests
   - `xunit`: 2.8.1 in Core.Tests, 2.7.1 in Api.Tests
   - `xunit.runner.visualstudio`: 2.8.1 in Core.Tests, 2.5.6 in Api.Tests

3. **Duplicated custom target** - `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted across all 3 source projects.

4. **Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property** - Set in all 3 source projects.

5. **Missing `Directory.Build.targets`** - The shared target could be centralized there instead of duplicated.

6. **Test projects repeat common test infrastructure** - Both test projects include the same testing package set (Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk, xunit, xunit.runner.visualstudio, NSubstitute).

7. **No global.json** - SDK version isn't pinned, which could lead to inconsistent builds across environments.

Now I'm ready to provide a comprehensive analysis and recommendations for consolidating these duplications.
